events.title: Movies in the Park
events.description:
Spend your summer nights under the stars at Movies in the Park! Held at Leif Erikson Park along the stunning shores of Lake Superior, this free, family-friendly event invites you to grab a blanket or lawn chair and enjoy a lineup of beloved films. Movies begin at sunset every Friday from mid-July to the end of August (2026 dates TBD). The 2025 lineup, chosen by public vote, includes favorites like Wicked (2024), The Parent Trap (1998), Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone (2001), and Ferris Bueller’s Day Off (1986).

Concessions will be available on-site, and leashed pets are welcome. Please note that parking is limited and all attendees under 16 must be accompanied by an adult. Movies will go on in light rain; check DowntownDuluth.com for any weather-related updates.
events.excerpt: Spend your summer nights under the stars at Movies in the Park! Held at Leif Erikson Park along the stunning shores of Lake Superior, this free, family-friendly event invites you to grab a blanket or lawn chair and enjoy a lineup of beloved films that begin playing at sunset.

venue.description: Originally Called Cullum Park and then Lake Shore Park, this greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek became Leif Erikson Park in 1927 after Bert Enger purchased the replica vessel Leif Erikson, which sailed from Norway to Duluth, and donated it to the city on the condition it be placed in the park and the park renamed in its honor. The park has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden and includes a historic amphitheater stage and the Lakewalk slices through it. The Leif Erikson vessel, however, sits in storage near Bayfront Park.
venue.excerpt: Leif Erikson Park, donated to the city of Duluth in 1927, is a greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek and has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden, including a historic amphitheater stage with a slice of the Lakewalk running through it.

organizer.description: Downtown Duluth’s Waterfront District is filled with vibrancy, history, and opportunity whether you’re a visitor or call Duluth home. Downtown Duluth helps make downtown stronger, cleaner, safer, and friendlier!

events.title: Duluth River Train Excursion
events.description:
Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife.

Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure.

Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

venue.description: Bayfront Festival Park is one of Duluth’s most beautiful public parks located on the shores of  Lake Superior.  Visitors to the park can view ships from around the world entering the harbor under the Aerial Lift Bridge. Bayfront Festival Park is home to a variety of events and activities throughout the year.  Enjoy an exciting array of festivals featuring music, entertainment, art and food.  During winter months, there is ice skating and a holiday lighting display. Take a walk around the festival grounds, stage and past the harbor. Play on the biggest, most innovative playground in Duluth. Bayfront Festival Park is a great place for people to go with friends and family almost any time.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

events.title: Sunday Night at the Races
events.description:
Join us (almost) every Sunday night in Proctor for the races!

Hear the rumble and feel the dust fly as our local dirt track racers put on a show for the fans all summer long! Bring your family to enjoy a Sunday evening outside with plenty of room in bleachers, concession stands, accessible fully running restrooms and a fun atmosphere. Grab a friend and try something new!

Check out our Facebook page to stay up to date with current events, cancellations due to weather and special events coming to our track now through September. (Search “Proctor KME Speedway” on Facebook.)

The cars hit the track at 4:30 to warm up, but the green flag drops at 5pm. Join us and help keep dirt track racing in the northland alive!

Admission (Regular Race Night)
Adults: $15
Students (13-18) & Senior Citizens: $10
Child (6-12): $2
Under 5: Free
Pit Admission: $30

Special Events
Saturday, August 1st: Midsummer Night of Mayhem School Bus Races (Rain day August 2nd.)
Sunday, August 23rd: Season Championship (Rain day August 30th.)
Thursday, September 3rd: 54th Annual Silver 1000 (Premiere racing event! Rain day September 9th.)
Wednesday, September 9th: Wednesday Nighters Car Show

events.title: High Key Mondays & Industry Nights
events.description:
High Key Mondays & Industry Night Are BACK!
Live local music | Drink specials | 6–9pm

High Key Monday is FRI-YAY for many of our industry partners — and we’re roll with it. Calling all restaurant, bar, liquor store, and brewery professionals in the Twin Ports — get on down to the Paddle for Industry Night!

Show a worker ID or pay stub for $1 off all Bent Paddle beverages. Thank you for keeping our paddles in the water!

Everyone is welcome! Come for the tunes, stay for the conversations, delicious beverages, and good times.

This month to kick off and celebrate the return, we invite you to connect with local organizations making waves in the Twin Ports — like WE Health, Trans+ Northland, Twin Ports Artists Collective, and Men As Peacemakers. Org’s may vary based on who available each week, but the good vibes are guaranteed!

events.title: $10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
events.description:
Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet!

Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out.

Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water.

Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

events.title: Concerts on the Pier
events.description:
Plan Now For A Perfect Summer Night at Glensheen

Celebrating its 11th anniversary in 2026, the Concerts on the Pier series is a FREE event every Wednesday, July – August. Bring a lawn chair or explore the grounds to find the perfect spot to enjoy music by local favorites. Come by car, foot or watercraft – all people and ages are welcome! Food and beverages are available for purchase. The event is free and open to the public from 5:30 to 8 pm. Gates open at 5 pm, however, for those interested, early access can be obtained by purchasing grounds admission or a tour. Each night, the opener will perform from 5:30-6 pm and the headliner will play from 6:30-8 pm.

At Concerts, guests can connect with neighbors and friends, play lawn games, grab some grub from a local food truck, enjoy a refreshing drink, eat locally-made ice cream, and take a seat by the shores of Lake Superior. Guests are encouraged to paddle or boat in to view the concert from the water, or are welcome to bring their own lawn chairs and blankets to enjoy the show from the shore. For each concert this year, Glensheen will also offer a shuttle bus (with a suggested donation) between the UMD campus and Glensheen for each concert this year. Parking at UMD is free in White Lot W during the summer.

2026 Lineup

July 1: Danny Frank & the Smoky Gold w/The Harlow Pennies
July 8: Slope City w/Water Cat
July 15: Joyann Parker w/Clayton Ryan
July 22: Sydney Hansen w/The Roadside Lupines
July 29: Maria & the Coins w/Dead End Friends
August 5: Lazerbeak w/Little Fevers
August 12: Climes w/Skarlett Woods
August 19: Brothers Burn Mountain w/Long Sleeves
August 26: Adam & The Dynamite Club w/Squid City Slingers

Food & Drinks

Three food truck options will be available each night for Concerts on the Pier: Johnny B’s, Bowlz n Thangz, Oasis Del Norte, UpDawg, and the Gumbeaux Guys. Local ice cream, small snacks, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks will also be available for purchase on-site.

Parking

Walk, bike, kayak, boat, ride share, or shuttle to Glensheen! Please avoid driving here if you can. LIMITED parking is available onsite, so those who plan to drive should arrive early. Glensheen does have a limited number of dedicated accessible parking spots available on-site for those with valid credentials. If you are driving here and plan to park off-site, please be kind to our neighbors and follow the City of Duluth’s parking rules. Learn more about the use of non-motorized vehicles on the Event Information page.

Shuttle Bus

Each night of Concerts on the Pier, Glensheen will be running a FREE* shuttle bus from UMD’s White Lot W (see map image on the Event Information page) down to Glensheen and back. Parking in this lot is FREE. The bus will be running on a continuous loop to and from Glensheen and UMD’s White Lot W between 5PM (first pick up at UMD) and 8:30PM (last boarding at Glensheen at 8PM). Riders can expect the bus every 20-30 minutes (with the first bus departing close to 5 pm from UMD), depending on traffic. If you plan to ride the bus, Glensheen kindly asks that you donate $5 per rider to the Concerts on the Pier fund to help support the ongoing operations of our nonprofit museum. We will have QR codes to scan on the bus and a physical donation receptacle at Glensheen for those wishing to donate with cash. This is a great option for folks looking to get dropped off right by the front gate!

events.title: Pier B Courtside Concerts
events.description:
June 17th: Summer Kickoff & 10 Year Celebration
Celebrate a decade of lakeside memories and the start of the best season of the year! We are thrilled to invite you to the Pier B Summer Kickoff Celebration on June 17th, a special edition of our Courtside Concerts honoring Pier B Resort Hotel’s 10-Year Anniversary. Set right on the water’s edge at the Pier B Sport Court, this evening promises the perfect Duluth summer vibe. Whether you’re here for the music by Aftershock, the BBQ and beverages, or the breathtaking harbor views, there’s no better place to ring in the season.

Live Music Most Wednesdays
Make plans for a summer night of live music on the water most Wednesdays! Grab your crew, order a drink, and enjoy the music with unbeatable views of Lake Superior. Check our event calendar for the full lineup.

We hope to see you there!

Food and beverage available for purchase.

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

venue.description: Experience the wonders of Lake Superior as we cruise next to 1,000-foot lake freighters and oceangoing vessels to take in the activities of the bustling harbor. The Vista Fleet is the Excursion Boat Company and (only) Floating Events Center based in Duluth, Minnesota. The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: Movies in the Park
events.description:
Spend your summer nights under the stars at Movies in the Park! Held at Leif Erikson Park along the stunning shores of Lake Superior, this free, family-friendly event invites you to grab a blanket or lawn chair and enjoy a lineup of beloved films. Movies begin at sunset every Friday from mid-July to the end of August (2026 dates TBD). The 2025 lineup, chosen by public vote, includes favorites like Wicked (2024), The Parent Trap (1998), Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone (2001), and Ferris Bueller’s Day Off (1986).

Concessions will be available on-site, and leashed pets are welcome. Please note that parking is limited and all attendees under 16 must be accompanied by an adult. Movies will go on in light rain; check DowntownDuluth.com for any weather-related updates.

venue.description: Originally Called Cullum Park and then Lake Shore Park, this greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek became Leif Erikson Park in 1927 after Bert Enger purchased the replica vessel Leif Erikson, which sailed from Norway to Duluth, and donated it to the city on the condition it be placed in the park and the park renamed in its honor. The park has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden and includes a historic amphitheater stage and the Lakewalk slices through it. The Leif Erikson vessel, however, sits in storage near Bayfront Park.
venue.excerpt: Leif Erikson Park, donated to the city of Duluth in 1927, is a greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek and has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden, including a historic amphitheater stage with a slice of the Lakewalk running through it.

organizer.description: Downtown Duluth’s Waterfront District is filled with vibrancy, history, and opportunity whether you’re a visitor or call Duluth home. The 90-block area features unique retail options, delicious restaurants, places to rest your head, and unforgettable sites to visit. Downtown Duluth helps make downtown stronger, cleaner, safer, and friendlier!

events.title: Duluth River Train Excursion
events.description:
Ride the Lake Superior and Mississippi Railroad. A historic 2-hour rail journey through the majestic St. Louis River Estuary. Family-friendly, volunteer-run, and purely Duluth. Our narrated tour takes you along the original Lake Superior & Mississippi line in vintage coaches. Go where cars and hiking boots can’t reach. The St. Louis River Estuary is a hidden sanctuary for bald eagles, osprey, and Northland wildlife.

Bring your camera for unparalleled views of the Duluth riverfront. Best to arrive about a half-hour before departure.

Our ticket office is located just off of Grand Avenue.

events.title: Sunday Night at the Races
events.description:
Join us (almost) every Sunday night in Proctor for the races!

Hear the rumble and feel the dust fly as our local dirt track racers put on a show for the fans all summer long! Bring your family to enjoy a Sunday evening outside with plenty of room in bleachers, concession stands, accessible fully running restrooms and a fun atmosphere. Grab a friend and try something new!

Check out our Facebook page to stay up to date with current events, cancellations due to weather and special events coming to our track now through September. (Search “Proctor KME Speedway” on Facebook.)

The cars hit the track at 4:30 to warm up, but the green flag drops at 5pm. Join us and help keep dirt track racing in the northland alive!

Admission (Regular Race Night)
Adults: $15
Students (13-18) & Senior Citizens: $10
Child (6-12): $2
Under 5: Free
Pit Admission: $30

Special Events
Saturday, August 1st: Midsummer Night of Mayhem School Bus Races (Rain day August 2nd.)
Sunday, August 23rd: Season Championship (Rain day August 30th.)
Thursday, September 3rd: 54th Annual Silver 1000 (Premiere racing event! Rain day September 9th.)
Wednesday, September 9th: Wednesday Nighters Car Show

events.title: Sunset Dinner Cruise on Lake Superior
events.description:
Experience Duluth from the water aboard a relaxing Sunset Dinner Cruise with Vista Fleet. Cruise through the Twin Ports harbor while taking in narrated highlights, harbor activity, and panoramic views of Duluth’s iconic skyline and the shores of Lake Superior.

Guests will enjoy a freshly prepared dinner catered by Black Woods Catering featuring your choice of entrée: Tuscan Chicken, Roasted Pork Tenderloin, or Butternut Squash Ravioli. Dinner also includes Black Woods House Salad, dinner rolls, Butter & Chive Whipped Potatoes, and Sautéed Green Beans Almondine.

Whether you’re celebrating a special occasion, entertaining out-of-town guests, or simply looking for a memorable night on the water, the Sunset Dinner Cruise offers one of Duluth’s most unique dining experiences.

Don’t forget to add dessert to your reservation for a sweet finish to the evening.

Cruises depart from the Duluth waterfront aboard the Vista Fleet.

The Fleet consists of: The Vista Star (100 ton vessel) that holds up to 220 passengers, The Vista Queen (46 ton vessel) that holds up to 50 passengers and the rebranded Zenith Adventure Gift Shop (located behind the DECC/Amsoil Arena) which is a 5,000 square gift shop and Adventure company. The cruises are fully live narrated with a blend of entertaining facts, games, concessions, a full bar and much more. Join us for a dinner, lunch, or family fun cruise!

events.title: High Key Mondays & Industry Nights
events.description:
High Key Mondays & Industry Night Are BACK!
Live local music | Drink specials | 6–9pm

High Key Monday is FRI-YAY for many of our industry partners — and we’re roll with it. Calling all restaurant, bar, liquor store, and brewery professionals in the Twin Ports — get on down to the Paddle for Industry Night!

Show a worker ID or pay stub for $1 off all Bent Paddle beverages. Thank you for keeping our paddles in the water!

Everyone is welcome! Come for the tunes, stay for the conversations, delicious beverages, and good times.

This month to kick off and celebrate the return, we invite you to connect with local organizations making waves in the Twin Ports — like WE Health, Trans+ Northland, Twin Ports Artists Collective, and Men As Peacemakers. Org’s may vary based on who available each week, but the good vibes are guaranteed!

events.title: TOAST – The Best of Bread
events.description:
TICKETS

Tickets on sale now!

 

ABOUT THE SHOW

Over their career, Bread scored 13 hits on Billboard’s Top 100! The two surviving members of Bread (David Gates & Robb Royer) are happily retired and enjoying their time with family and the fruits of their musical careers. Enter TOAST – The Ultimate Bread Experience who does everything possible to keep the legacy of Bread alive.

With a surprising fidelity rarely heard from a live tribute band, their sound is often described as “spot on” to the original recordings. Audiences everywhere rave about closely their live performances sound like the cherished records they listened to back in the day.

TOAST will take you on a journey back in time, engaging a spectrum of emotions; from the tender moments that tug on the heartstrings to the upbeat classics that compel you to jump out of your seat and dance. And when combined with truly timeless music, this concert will “rise” to the top as one of your absolute favorite concerts of all time!

 

 

KNOW BEFORE YOU GO

events.title: $10 Tuesday on the Vista Fleet
events.description:
Climb onboard for one of Duluth’s favorite summer traditions, $10 Tuesdays are back at the Vista Fleet!

Enjoy live-narrated sightseeing cruises through the Duluth-Superior Harbor for just $10 per ticket, all day long, every Tuesday through Labor Day. Reserve your tickets in advance as tours DO sell out.

Take in stunning views of Lake Superior, the Aerial Lift Bridge, and the working waterfront from the comfort of the Vista Fleet. Concessions and a full bar are available onboard, so grab a drink, kick back, and enjoy a midweek escape on the water.

Book your tickets online, no promo code needed!

events.title: Concerts on the Pier
events.description:
Plan Now For A Perfect Summer Night at Glensheen

Celebrating its 11th anniversary in 2026, the Concerts on the Pier series is a FREE event every Wednesday, July – August. Bring a lawn chair or explore the grounds to find the perfect spot to enjoy music by local favorites. Come by car, foot or watercraft – all people and ages are welcome! Food and beverages are available for purchase. The event is free and open to the public from 5:30 to 8 pm. Gates open at 5 pm, however, for those interested, early access can be obtained by purchasing grounds admission or a tour. Each night, the opener will perform from 5:30-6 pm and the headliner will play from 6:30-8 pm.

At Concerts, guests can connect with neighbors and friends, play lawn games, grab some grub from a local food truck, enjoy a refreshing drink, eat locally-made ice cream, and take a seat by the shores of Lake Superior. Guests are encouraged to paddle or boat in to view the concert from the water, or are welcome to bring their own lawn chairs and blankets to enjoy the show from the shore. For each concert this year, Glensheen will also offer a shuttle bus (with a suggested donation) between the UMD campus and Glensheen for each concert this year. Parking at UMD is free in White Lot W during the summer.

2026 Lineup

July 1: Danny Frank & the Smoky Gold w/The Harlow Pennies
July 8: Slope City w/Water Cat
July 15: Joyann Parker w/Clayton Ryan
July 22: Sydney Hansen w/The Roadside Lupines
July 29: Maria & the Coins w/Dead End Friends
August 5: Lazerbeak w/Little Fevers
August 12: Climes w/Skarlett Woods
August 19: Brothers Burn Mountain w/Long Sleeves
August 26: Adam & The Dynamite Club w/Squid City Slingers

Food & Drinks

Three food truck options will be available each night for Concerts on the Pier: Johnny B’s, Bowlz n Thangz, Oasis Del Norte, UpDawg, and the Gumbeaux Guys. Local ice cream, small snacks, and alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks will also be available for purchase on-site.

Parking

Walk, bike, kayak, boat, ride share, or shuttle to Glensheen! Please avoid driving here if you can. LIMITED parking is available onsite, so those who plan to drive should arrive early. Glensheen does have a limited number of dedicated accessible parking spots available on-site for those with valid credentials. If you are driving here and plan to park off-site, please be kind to our neighbors and follow the City of Duluth’s parking rules. Learn more about the use of non-motorized vehicles on the Event Information page.

Shuttle Bus

Each night of Concerts on the Pier, Glensheen will be running a FREE* shuttle bus from UMD’s White Lot W (see map image on the Event Information page) down to Glensheen and back. Parking in this lot is FREE. The bus will be running on a continuous loop to and from Glensheen and UMD’s White Lot W between 5PM (first pick up at UMD) and 8:30PM (last boarding at Glensheen at 8PM). Riders can expect the bus every 20-30 minutes (with the first bus departing close to 5 pm from UMD), depending on traffic. If you plan to ride the bus, Glensheen kindly asks that you donate $5 per rider to the Concerts on the Pier fund to help support the ongoing operations of our nonprofit museum. We will have QR codes to scan on the bus and a physical donation receptacle at Glensheen for those wishing to donate with cash. This is a great option for folks looking to get dropped off right by the front gate!

events.title: Pier B Courtside Concerts
events.description:
June 17th: Summer Kickoff & 10 Year Celebration
Celebrate a decade of lakeside memories and the start of the best season of the year! We are thrilled to invite you to the Pier B Summer Kickoff Celebration on June 17th, a special edition of our Courtside Concerts honoring Pier B Resort Hotel’s 10-Year Anniversary. Set right on the water’s edge at the Pier B Sport Court, this evening promises the perfect Duluth summer vibe. Whether you’re here for the music by Aftershock, the BBQ and beverages, or the breathtaking harbor views, there’s no better place to ring in the season.

Live Music Most Wednesdays
Make plans for a summer night of live music on the water most Wednesdays! Grab your crew, order a drink, and enjoy the music with unbeatable views of Lake Superior. Check our event calendar for the full lineup.

We hope to see you there!

Food and beverage available for purchase.

events.title: Movies in the Park
events.description:
Spend your summer nights under the stars at Movies in the Park! Held at Leif Erikson Park along the stunning shores of Lake Superior, this free, family-friendly event invites you to grab a blanket or lawn chair and enjoy a lineup of beloved films. Movies begin at sunset every Friday from mid-July to the end of August (2026 dates TBD). The 2025 lineup, chosen by public vote, includes favorites like Wicked (2024), The Parent Trap (1998), Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone (2001), and Ferris Bueller’s Day Off (1986).

Concessions will be available on-site, and leashed pets are welcome. Please note that parking is limited and all attendees under 16 must be accompanied by an adult. Movies will go on in light rain; check DowntownDuluth.com for any weather-related updates.

venue.description: Originally Called Cullum Park and then Lake Shore Park, this greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek became Leif Erikson Park in 1927 after Bert Enger purchased the replica vessel Leif Erikson, which sailed from Norway to Duluth, and donated it to the city on the condition it be placed in the park and the park renamed in its honor. The park has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden and includes a historic amphitheater stage and the Lakewalk slices through it. The Leif Erikson vessel, however, sits in storage near Bayfront Park.
venue.excerpt: Leif Erikson Park, donated to the city of Duluth in 1927, is a greenspace located along Lake Superior near the mouth of Chester Creek and has long been home to Duluth’s Rose Garden, including a historic amphitheater stage with a slice of the Lakewalk running through it.

Downtown Duluth helps make downtown stronger, cleaner, safer, and friendlier!
